items.sigma_rule:
title: SENTINEL APEX — Critical Netty HTTP2 Advertised MAX_CONCURRENT_STREAMS are not enforced CVE-2023
id: 805005ae-c1ec-5257-b8a6-83d10940b5de
status: experimental
description: |
    Detects exploitation attempts related to CVE-2023-44487 (Web Application).
    Generated by CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX v166.2.
    Severity: CRITICAL | Class: RCE
references:
    - https://intel.cyberdudebivash.com
    - https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2023-44487
author: CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX Detection Engineering
date: 2026/06/13
modified: 2026/06/13
tags:
    - attack.initial_access
logsource:
    category: process_creation
    product: generic
detection:
    selection_process:
        CommandLine|contains:
            - 'cmd.exe /c'
            - 'powershell -enc'
            - '/bin/sh -c'
            - 'wget http'
            - 'curl http'
    selection_webshell:
        Image|endswith:
            - '\cmd.exe'
            - '\powershell.exe'
            - '/bash'
            - '/sh'
        ParentImage|contains:
            - 'w3wp.exe'
            - 'httpd'
            - 'nginx'
            - 'apache2'
            - 'tomcat'
    condition: selection_process or selection_webshell
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative scripts executing via web server
    - Deployment automation tools
level: critical
items.kql_query:
// SENTINEL APEX — CVE-2023-44487 Remote Code Execution Detection
// Product: Web Application | Generated: 2026-06-13T08:14:21Z
// Deploy to: Microsoft Sentinel / Defender XDR
let lookback = 1d;
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
| where (InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("w3wp.exe", "httpd.exe", "nginx.exe", "tomcat.exe", "java.exe")
    and FileName in~ ("cmd.exe", "powershell.exe", "bash", "sh", "python.exe", "python3"))
    or (ProcessCommandLine has_any ("wget http", "curl http", "certutil -urlcache", "bitsadmin /transfer"))
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine,
          InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine
| where not(ProcessCommandLine has_any ("update", "install", "backup", "deploy"))  // filter known-good
| extend Severity = "HIGH", CVE = "CVE-2023-44487", RuleSource = "SENTINEL-APEX"
| order by Timestamp desc
items.suricata_rule: alert http $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HTTP_SERVERS $HTTP_PORTS (msg:"SENTINEL-APEX CVE-2023-44487 Exploitation Attempt - RCE"; flow:established,to_server; content:"/bin/sh"; nocase; content:"cmd.exe"; nocase; threshold:type threshold, track by_src, count 3, seconds 60; classtype:web-application-attack; sid:6955809; rev:1; metadata:created_at 2026-06-13, source SENTINEL-APEX-v166.2;)
items.apex_ai_summary: [P1] CVE / Vulnerability - moderate severity (Risk 5.0/10). EPSS 94.39% exploitation probability (30-day forecast). MITRE ATT&CK mapped: Exploit Public-Facing Application, Command and Scripting Interpreter (2 techniques). Actor attribution: CDB-UNATTR-CVE. AI confidence: LOW (35%) - limited source data available.

author: CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX Detection Engineering
date: 2026/06/13
modified: 2026/06/13
tags:
    - attack.initial_access
logsource:
    category: network_connection
    product: generic
detection:
    selection:
        EventID:
            - 4625
            - 4648

        LogonType: 3
    condition: selection
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative access

level: medium
items.kql_query:
// SENTINEL APEX — CVE-2026-0714 — Anomalous Activity Detection
// Product: Web Application | Generated: 2026-06-13T08:14:21Z
let lookback = 1d;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
| where EventID in (4625, 4648, 4728, 4740, 4776)
| summarize FailureCount = count(), DistinctTargets = dcount(TargetUserName)
    by IpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| where FailureCount > 10 or DistinctTargets > 5
| extend Severity = "MEDIUM", CVE = "CVE-2026-0714", RuleSource = "SENTINEL-APEX"
| order by TimeGenerated desc
items.suricata_rule: alert http $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HTTP_SERVERS $HTTP_PORTS (msg:"SENTINEL-APEX CVE-2026-0714 Exploitation Attempt - GENERIC"; flow:established,to_server; content:"|90 90 90 90|"; rawbytes; threshold:type threshold, track by_src, count 3, seconds 60; classtype:web-application-attack; sid:9250022; rev:1; metadata:created_at 2026-06-13, source SENTINEL-APEX-v166.2;)

items.title: Fortinet patched a new critical FortiSandbox flaw
items.description: Fortinet patched a critical FortiSandbox vulnerability that could let unauthenticated attackers remotely execute commands via crafted HTTP requests. Fortinet released security updates to address several vulnerabilities affecting FortiSandbox, FortiOS, FortiProxy, and FortiPortal. The most severe issue, tracked as CVE-2026-25089 (CVSS score of 9.8), is an OS command injection flaw in FortiSandbox products. The vulnerability could […]

items.title: Medium: Apostrophe has a Weak Password Recovery Mechanism for Forgotten Password and Imp (CVE-2026-45013)
items.description: CVE-2026-45013 - Apostrophe has a Weak Password Recovery Mechanism for Forgotten Password and Improper Input Validation

apex.ai_summary: [P2] CVE / Vulnerability - moderate severity (Risk 6.2/10). EPSS 0.14% exploitation probability (30-day forecast). MITRE ATT&CK mapped: Exploit Public-Facing Application, Command and Scripting Interpreter, Valid Accounts, Phishing (4 techniques). Actor attribution: CDB-UNATTR-CVE. AI confidence: LOW (31%) - limited source data available.
apex.recommended_action: HIGH PRIORITY - Patch within 72 hours. Monitor affected assets. Review threat hunting queries for related TTPs.

items.nvd_disclosure: This CVE ID was not found in the NIST NVD database at time of enrichment. Intelligence is preliminary and unverified by NVD. CVSS score and severity are analyst-estimated until NVD confirmation.
items.sigma_rule:
title: SENTINEL APEX — Medium Apostrophe has a Weak Password Recovery Mechanism for Forgotten Password 
id: 94bf600e-0eba-592f-b627-4946b7b59d3b
status: experimental
description: |
    Detects exploitation attempts related to CVE-2026-45013 (Web Application).
    Generated by CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX v166.2.
    Severity: MEDIUM | Class: RCE
references:
    - https://intel.cyberdudebivash.com
    - https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-45013
author: CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX Detection Engineering
date: 2026/06/13
modified: 2026/06/13
tags:
    - attack.initial_access
logsource:
    category: process_creation
    product: generic
detection:
    selection_process:
        CommandLine|contains:
            - 'cmd.exe /c'
            - 'powershell -enc'
            - '/bin/sh -c'
            - 'wget http'
            - 'curl http'
    selection_webshell:
        Image|endswith:
            - '\cmd.exe'
            - '\powershell.exe'
            - '/bash'
            - '/sh'
        ParentImage|contains:
            - 'w3wp.exe'
            - 'httpd'
            - 'nginx'
            - 'apache2'
            - 'tomcat'
    condition: selection_process or selection_webshell
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative scripts executing via web server
    - Deployment automation tools
level: medium
items.kql_query:
// SENTINEL APEX — CVE-2026-45013 Remote Code Execution Detection
// Product: Web Application | Generated: 2026-06-13T08:14:21Z
// Deploy to: Microsoft Sentinel / Defender XDR
let lookback = 1d;
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
| where (InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("w3wp.exe", "httpd.exe", "nginx.exe", "tomcat.exe", "java.exe")
    and FileName in~ ("cmd.exe", "powershell.exe", "bash", "sh", "python.exe", "python3"))
    or (ProcessCommandLine has_any ("wget http", "curl http", "certutil -urlcache", "bitsadmin /transfer"))
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine,
          InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine
| where not(ProcessCommandLine has_any ("update", "install", "backup", "deploy"))  // filter known-good
| extend Severity = "HIGH", CVE = "CVE-2026-45013", RuleSource = "SENTINEL-APEX"
| order by Timestamp desc
items.suricata_rule: alert http $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HTTP_SERVERS $HTTP_PORTS (msg:"SENTINEL-APEX CVE-2026-45013 Exploitation Attempt - RCE"; flow:established,to_server; content:"/bin/sh"; nocase; content:"cmd.exe"; nocase; threshold:type threshold, track by_src, count 3, seconds 60; classtype:web-application-attack; sid:9466532; rev:1; metadata:created_at 2026-06-13, source SENTINEL-APEX-v166.2;)

items.title: Russian APTs Still Exploiting Patched WinRAR Flaw CVE-2025-8088
items.description: Despite a 2025 patch, Russian-linked groups still exploit a WinRAR flaw (CVE-2025-8088) to deploy malware via phishing archives. CVE-2025-8088 is a path traversal flaw in WinRAR that lets an attacker write files outside the extraction directory using NTFS Alternate Data Streams. WinRAR fixed it in version 7.13 in July 2025. Nearly a year later, Trend […]
items.apex_ai_summary: [HIGH] Threat Intel advisory. Risk score 7.5/10. Full APEX AI analysis available to Pro subscribers.

items.title: File Browser: Symlink following lets scoped users read, overwrite, and share files outside their filebrowser scope
items.description:
## Summary

File Browser enforces per-user scope with `afero.NewBasePathFs(afero.NewOsFs(), scope)`, set up in `users/users.go`. This blocks lexical `../` traversal, but it does not stop the HTTP file handlers from following symbolic links before they open, serve, write, share, or list a file. As a result, a scoped user — and in some cases an unauthenticated public-share recipient — can cross the intended scope boundary by following a symlink whose path is lexically inside their scope but whose target is outside it.

Two distinct shapes are covered here:

- **Variant 1 — symlink as the final path component.** A symlink that lives inside the user's scoped tree and points to a file under the server root but outside the scope. The handlers record the symlink (`IsSymlink`) but then resolve and operate on the target anyway.
- **Variant 2 — file or directory reached through a symlinked ancestor.** A regular file requested *through* a symlinked directory.

Read, write (including TUS resumable
